summary:
Endoscopy is preferred for diagnosing Zenker’s diverticulum because it provides definitive visual confirmation while allowing simultaneous intervention if needed; no other investigation offers these combined benefits effectively. The best option remains
\boxed{B}. Agent1 concluded by emphasizing why an endoscope was superior over alternatives like a barium swallow test when dealing with potential cases of Zenker's Diverticulum based purely around efficiency and effectiveness at providing accurate diagnosis alongside enabling timely treatments through one procedure rather than multiple steps required from less versatile methods. This ensured patient care remained streamlined without unnecessary delays between assessment phases leading towards appropriate therapy choices being made quickly after identification confirmed via endoscopic means alone suffices here given all factors considered together logically pointing toward selection 'B'.
